title: rosa woodsii var. neomexicana
intro: Rosa woodsii var. neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico rose, is a variety of the Rocky Mountain rose plant that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The New Mexico rose is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
